title: Two Gaps Between Developers and AI
text: Many people are anxious about whether AI will replace programmers. The real question is not whether AI can write code, but whether developers can clearly define what needs to be built. The first gap is the specification gap. In the past, developers could start coding with vague requirements and figure things out along the way. With AI, you need to make a decision about exactly what you want before the AI generates anything. The second gap is the verification gap. When AI writes code, someone still needs to review it. Making decisions about code quality, security and performance requires deep expertise. The bottom line is that AI amplifies the skills you already have. If you can think clearly and communicate precisely, AI becomes a powerful tool. If not, it just helps you make mistakes faster.
zh: 开发者与 AI 之间的两个差距 本文讨论 AI 编程时代中，任务定义和代码验证为什么变得更重要。 很多人担心 AI 是否会取代程序员。真正的问题不是 AI 能不能写代码，而是开发者能不能清楚地定义需要构建什么。 第一个差距是需求定义差距。过去开发者可以用模糊的需求开始编码，边做边想。有了 AI，你需要在 AI 生成任何东西之前就明确做出决定。 第二个差距是验证差距。当 AI 写代码时，仍然需要有人来审查。对代码质量、安全性和性能做出判断需要深厚的专业知识。 底线是 AI 放大了你已有的技能。如果你能清晰思考并精确沟通，AI 就成了强大的工具。否则，它只会帮你更快地犯错。

title: Education & Learning
text: Technology is changing how students learn in classrooms around the world. Many schools now use artificial intelligence tools to help teachers plan lessons and track student progress. However, not everyone agrees that more technology means better education. A recent report from Beijing Normal University suggests that science education should focus on building critical thinking, not just memorizing facts. The report argues that students need to learn how to ask questions and solve real-world problems, rather than simply following instructions. In Shanghai, young students are already leading the way. Some teenagers have discovered new insect species, while others are developing smart devices to help elderly people. These projects show that hands-on experience can be more powerful than traditional textbooks. The challenge now is teacher training. Many science teachers feel unprepared for the new demands. Schools need to invest in professional development so that teachers can guide students through this changing landscape.
zh: 教育与学习 教育与学习 — education, technology, growth 科技正在改变世界各地课堂中学生学习的方式。现在许多学校使用人工智能工具帮助教师备课和跟踪学生学习进度。然而，并非所有人都认为更多科技意味着更好的教育。 北京师范大学最近的一份报告建议，科学教育应注重培养批判性思维，而不仅仅是记忆事实。报告认为，学生需要学会如何提出问题和解决现实问题，而不是简单地遵循指令。 在上海，年轻学生已经走在前列。一些青少年发现了新的昆虫物种，另一些则在开发帮助老年人的智能设备。这些项目表明，动手实践可能比传统教科书更有力量。 现在的挑战是教师培训。许多科学教师对新要求感到准备不足。学校需要投资专业发展，以便教师能够引导学生在不断变化的环境中前行。

title: Environment & Climate
text: Climate change is one of the most pressing challenges of our time. Rising global temperatures have led to more frequent extreme weather events, including hurricanes, droughts, and floods. The burning of fossil fuels such as coal, oil, and natural gas is the primary driver of greenhouse gas emissions, which trap heat in the atmosphere and accelerate global warming. Deforestation further exacerbates the problem by reducing the planet's ability to absorb carbon dioxide. In response, many countries have committed to reducing their carbon footprint through renewable energy sources like wind and solar power. The transition to a low-carbon economy requires significant investment in clean technology and infrastructure. Individual actions, such as reducing waste, conserving energy, and supporting sustainable practices, also play a crucial role. The concept of a circular economy, where materials are reused and recycled rather than discarded, is gaining traction as a way to minimize environmental impact. Biodiversity loss is another critical concern, as species extinction disrupts ecosystems and threatens food security. International cooperation is essential to address these global challenges, as no single country can solve them alone.
zh: 环境与气候 环境与气候 — environment, climate, sustainability 气候变化是我们这个时代最紧迫的挑战之一。全球气温上升导致了更频繁的极端天气事件，包括飓风、干旱和洪水。煤炭、石油和天然气等化石燃料的燃烧是温室气体排放的主要驱动因素，温室气体在大气中困住热量并加速全球变暖。 森林砍伐进一步加剧了这一问题，降低了地球吸收二氧化碳的能力。作为回应，许多国家已承诺通过风能和太阳能等可再生能源来减少碳足迹。向低碳经济转型需要大量投资清洁技术和基础设施。 个人行动，如减少废物、节约能源和支持可持续实践，也发挥着至关重要的作用。循环经济的概念正在获得关注，即材料被重复使用和回收而不是被丢弃，这是最大限度减少环境影响的一种方式。生物多样性丧失是另一个关键问题，因为物种灭绝破坏生态系统并威胁粮食安全。 国际合作对于应对这些全球挑战至关重要，因为没有任何一个国家能够单独解决这些问题。

title: Software Engineering
text: Software engineering is the discipline of designing, building, and maintaining reliable software systems. Unlike simple coding, it involves careful planning, systematic testing, and ongoing maintenance. One of the core principles is separation of concerns: breaking a complex system into smaller, manageable modules. Each module should have a single, well-defined responsibility. This approach makes the code easier to test, debug, and extend over time. Version control systems like Git allow teams to track changes, collaborate on the same codebase, and revert to previous versions when something goes wrong. Code reviews serve as a quality gate where experienced developers examine new code before it is merged. Automated testing, including unit tests and integration tests, ensures that changes do not introduce unexpected bugs. Documentation is another critical but often overlooked aspect. Well-written documentation helps new team members understand the system and reduces the time spent on onboarding. In modern software engineering, continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) automate the process of building, testing, and releasing software, enabling teams to deliver updates faster and with greater confidence.
zh: 软件工程 软件工程 — software, engineering, development 软件工程是设计、构建和维护可靠软件系统的学科。与简单编码不同，它涉及仔细的规划、系统化的测试和持续的维护。其核心原则之一是关注点分离：将复杂系统拆分为更小、可管理的模块。 每个模块应该有单一的、明确的职责。这种方法使代码更容易测试、调试和扩展。像Git这样的版本控制系统允许团队跟踪更改、在同一个代码库上协作，并在出错时回滚到之前的版本。 代码审查作为质量关卡，由有经验的开发者在合并新代码之前进行检查。自动化测试，包括单元测试和集成测试，确保更改不会引入意外的bug。文档是另一个关键但常被忽视的方面。 编写良好的文档帮助新团队成员理解系统，减少入职时间。在现代软件工程中，持续集成和持续部署自动化了构建、测试和发布软件的过程，使团队能够更快、更有信心地交付更新。
